text: Any motor vehicle owned and operated by a school district with an average daily attendance in excess of 400,000 while being used to measure the distance from school to a school pupil’s residence may display a flashing amber warning light to the rear of the vehicle when moving at a speed substantially slower than the normal flow of traffic.

# Vehicle Code
## Division 12. EQUIPMENT OF VEHICLES [24000 - 28244]
### Chapter 2. Lighting Equipment [24250 - 26106]
#### Article 7. Flashing and Colored Lights [25250 - 25282]
##### § 25273

Any motor vehicle owned and operated by a school district with an average daily attendance in excess of 400,000 while being used to measure the distance from school to a school pupil’s residence may display a flashing amber warning light to the rear of the vehicle when moving at a speed substantially slower than the normal flow of traffic.

*(Added by Stats. 1963, Ch. 404.)*

subsections.text: Any motor vehicle owned and operated by a school district with an average daily attendance in excess of 400,000 while being used to measure the distance from school to a school pupil’s residence may display a flashing amber warning light to the rear of the vehicle when moving at a speed substantially slower than the normal flow of traffic.
